_comment: HELD KEY for the novelty-reserve pressure. A SEPARATE file the measurement never reads. It carries the item identities, the predicted per-channel dissociation, the control's predicted behaviour, and the mechanism tag. The scorer reads this AFTER the blind measurement has emitted its channels.
claim: The Bayesian-surprise a newcomer produces tracks the recent NOVELTY RATE (how often newcomers have lately arrived), not just the standing mass or the bare fact of being new. A newcomer after a long confirmation stretch (drought) moves belief MORE than the same newcomer after a flurry of newcomers, when standing mass is matched. The hand-rolled constant reserve (NOVELTY_RESERVE = 1.0) cannot tell drought from flurry; the signal-derived reserve (the γ-decayed first-appearance mass, ν) can.
mechanism: surpriseAt's reserve AMPLITUDE. Under signalReserve the reserve probability ν/(sumPrior+ν) is fed the γ-decayed novelty rate ν instead of the constant 1.0, then run through the SAME fixed Born step. reserve_prob is high after a flurry (ν≈sumPrior → newcomer is expected → small KL) and low after a drought (ν→0 → newcomer is unexpected → large KL).

seeds_of_record.title: Under the Lantern
seeds_of_record.supplies: the phenomenon (a named cast entering a scene) and the names Arna, Dammann, Wieman, Lamprecht

s1.predicted: LOW novelty rate at test → LARGE bayes under signalReserve

s2.predicted: HIGH novelty rate at test → SMALL bayes under signalReserve; standing mass matched to s1

s3.predicted: re-confirm at test (no newcomer); test line is BYTE-IDENTICAL surface to s1's test line. bayes ≈ 0 under BOTH reserves — the loud-surface null

t1.predicted: tonal drought (one pitch recurs, then a new pitch) → LARGE bayes under signalReserve

t2.predicted: tonal flurry (six distinct pitches, then a new pitch) → SMALL bayes under signalReserve; matched mass

t3.predicted: tonal re-confirm (same pitch) → bayes ≈ 0 under BOTH reserves

dissociation.OFF: constant reserve: bayes(drought) ≈ bayes(flurry) — BLIND to the novelty rate (matched standing mass); control ≈ 0
dissociation.ON: signal reserve: bayes(drought) ≫ bayes(flurry) — the rate is tracked; bayes(drought) ≫ control; flurry stays small
dissociation.surface_controls_held: surprisal (token improbability) is IDENTICAL between drought and flurry, and standing mass (OFF bayes) is identical — so no surface feature can produce the drought≫flurry split. Only the signal reserve does.
dissociation.omnimodal: the same split appears on the tonal basis (t1≫t2 under ON, t1≈t2 under OFF), driven by the SAME core recurrence — so the capability is interior, not a text fact
